mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in real retail sales growth reflects resilient household demand and near-term earnings support, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the sales trend is positive.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS trend states do not deliver higher 20-day SPX returns than negative trend states in walk-forward tests, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 5.0%, high short rates raise equity discount rates and tighten financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS-above-5.0 episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observations or average triggered returns exceed the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate combined with a high federal funds rate marks late-cycle overheating, where policy restraint threatens margins and demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint UNRATE-below-4 and FEDFUNDS-above-4.5 condition has no incremental negative predictive effect versus either proxy alone, or 60-day hit rate for negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production crossing above 103 signals broadening real activity and operating leverage for cyclical firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO-above-103 crossing episodes fail to outperform unconditional 60-day SPX returns by at least 50 basis points on average in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1500 indicate weakening rate-sensitive demand and reduced future construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-1500 triggered observations produce positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or have average returns above unconditional 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI while the federal funds rate is already above 4.5% signals sticky inflation and prolonged restrictive policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month change accelerates and FEDFUNDS above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI acceleration and FEDFUNDS-above-4.5 interaction has a nonnegative coefficient in walk-forward return regressions or negative-return hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth with unemployment below 4.0% signals strong income formation without visible labor-market stress, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE below 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS growth with UNRATE below 4.0% does not raise 5-day forward SPX returns relative to baseline or if the walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth above its prior-year level confirms broad demand expansion and supports aggregate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive year-over-year GDPC1 states have lower average 60-day SPX returns than nonpositive states in walk-forward tests, or if the sign accuracy is below 50%.
